quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 11 Quiz 9
quiz.subtitle: 11.9: Simple Harmonic Motion
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Using trig functions to model cyclic phenomena

questions.question: A mass on a spring oscillates according to $d = 5\cos(2t)$, where $d$ is displacement in cm and $t$ is time in seconds. What is the maximum distance the mass moves from its equilibrium position?
questions.explanation: The amplitude is the absolute value of the lead coefficient: $|5| = 5$ cm.

questions.question: If a pendulum has a period of $4$ seconds, what is its frequency in cycles per second (Hz)?
questions.explanation: Frequency is the reciprocal of the period: $f = 1/T = 1/4 = 0.25$ Hz.

questions.question: If a buoy's height is modeled by $h = 3\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}t)$, how long does it take for the buoy to complete one full cycle?
A mass oscillates according to $d = 6\sin(4\pi t)$. Find the frequency in Hz.
A tuning fork produces a sound wave with a frequency of $440$ Hz. What is the period of this sound wave?
questions.explanation: Period is the reciprocal of frequency: $T = 1/f = 1/440$ seconds.

questions.question: If a metronome's arm follows $d = 4\cos(8\pi t)$, how many full swings (cycles) does it complete in one second?
An object in simple harmonic motion has amplitude $7$ cm. Find the total distance traveled in one complete period.
questions.explanation: In one cycle: equilibrium $\to$ max ($a$) $\to$ equilibrium ($a$) $\to$ min ($a$) $\to$ equilibrium ($a$). Total distance = $4a = 4 \times 7 = 28$ cm.

questions.question: An object takes $0.5$ seconds to go from its highest point to its lowest point. What is the period of the motion?
questions.explanation: Highest to lowest is half a cycle. Full period = $0.5 \times 2 = 1.0$ second.

questions.question: An object in SHM has amplitude $8$ cm and period $\pi$ seconds. It starts at the midline moving upward. Write the equation.
questions.explanation: Start at midline moving up $\to$ sine. Period $\pi \to b = 2\pi/\pi = 2$. Amplitude $8$. $y = 8\sin(2t)$.

questions.question: An object has frequency $5$ Hz and amplitude $2$ cm, starting at equilibrium moving upward. What is the equation of motion?
questions.explanation: Start at equilibrium $\to$ sine. $f = 5 \to b = 2\pi f = 10\pi$. Amplitude $2$. $y = 2\sin(10\pi t)$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a great feel for the physics of trig functions — amplitude, period, frequency, total distance per cycle, writing equations from descriptions, and working with harmonic motion models.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Key reminders: (1) Period $T = 2\pi/b$, Frequency $f = 1/T = b/(2\pi)$, (2) Total distance in one period = $4a$, (3) Starting at max $\to$ cosine; starting at midline moving up $\to$ sine, (4) Highest to lowest is half a period.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Extracting $b$ from the equation and using it to find period and frequency, (2) Writing equations: identify amplitude, determine sine or cosine from starting position, compute $b$ from period or frequency, (3) The relationship $f = 1/T$ is key — they are reciprocals.
scoreMessages.0: Review simple harmonic motion: (1) $y = a\sin(bt)$ or $y = a\cos(bt)$ models oscillation, (2) Amplitude $|a|$ = maximum displacement from equilibrium, (3) Period $T = 2\pi/b$, Frequency $f = 1/T = b/(2\pi)$, (4) Start at max $\to$ cosine; start at midline moving up $\to$ sine.
